steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This "find supporting evidence" question asks which finding most directly supports the researchers' conclusion that the transition from a stagnant lid regime to a tectonic plate regime occurred at 3.2 billion years ago. The decisive clue is the mechanism: before the transition, lithosphere and mantle were completely isolated; after it, their material began to mix. Supporting evidence must show mixing beginning at that specific date.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Among rocks known to be older than 3.2 billion years, significantly more are mantle derived than lithospheric, but the opposite is true for the rocks younger than 3.2 billion years.": The text gives no basis for comparing quantities of rock types; sheer abundance tells us nothing about whether mixing occurred. ✗
  • "Mantle-derived rocks older than 3.2 billion years show significantly more compositional diversity than lithospheric rocks older than 3.2 billion years do.": Comparing the diversity of two separate rock categories does not show that material transferred between them; this leaves the mixing question unanswered. ✗
  • "There is a positive correlation between the age of lithospheric rocks and their chemical similarity to mantle-derived rocks, and that correlation increases significantly in strength at around 3.2 billion years old.": This would mean the oldest lithospheric rocks are the most similar to mantle rocks, contradicting the claim that lithosphere and mantle were isolated before 3.2 billion years ago; the direction is backwards. ✗
  • "Mantle-derived rocks younger than 3.2 billion years contain some material that is not found in older mantle-derived rocks but is found in older and contemporaneous lithospheric rocks.": This is the chemical fingerprint of mixing. The foreign material appears in young mantle rocks (after the transition) but not in old ones (before it), directly confirming that lithospheric material began entering the mantle at 3.2 billion years ago. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
